title: Overhead (Top-Down) View Toggle
content:
## Overview

The snake game board supports an overhead (top-down) view mode, toggled by pressing `O`. When active, the isometric 3D perspective is fully flattened into a simple axis-aligned top-down layout. Pressing `O` again restores the previous view exactly.

## Implementation

All logic lives in `src/main.ts`.

### `toggleOverheadView()`

The entry point for the feature. On activation it:

1. Saves the current isometric angle, perspective ratio, and perspective strength to private fields.
2. Zeroes all three values to produce a flat, axis-aligned view.

On deactivation it restores the saved values exactly, so any customisation the user made before pressing `O` is preserved.

### Flat block rendering

Zeroing the camera angles alone was insufficient — snake segments and food blocks still rendered as extruded cubes viewed straight-on. The fix makes `baseBlockHeight` conditional on the overhead flag: when overhead view is active, block height is set to zero, causing all blocks to render as flat squares. This is a single-expression change, keeping the fix minimal and contained.

### Input lockout

While overhead view is active:

- Rotation keys (`Q`/`E`) return early without effect.
- Perspective keys (`[`/`]`) return early without effect.
- The auto-rotate loop skips its angle update.

This keeps the overhead view stable and predictable. The alternative — allowing those keys to work and immediately re-flattening — was rejected as confusing.

### Controls text

Controls text in all three game modes (single-player, PvP, BvB) was updated to document the `O` key.

## Key Binding

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `O` | Toggle overhead (top-down) view |
